acceptance_targets: [CH18_PROSE_POV_RUPTURE] Pass if the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:3 establishes a single explicit access model and the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:11; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:21; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:33; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:45 contain only directly observable detail or clearly sourced document language. Fail if the chapter still jumps between outsiders' private histories or interior reactions without framing.

[CH18_CRAFT_BODY_COUNT_CONTRADICTION] Pass if a reader can add the revised location counts in the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:27 and reach exactly 30 without inferring hidden exclusions. Fail if the arithmetic on the page can still produce any number other than 30.

[CH18_CRAFT_RELAY_CONTRADICTION] Pass if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:63 preserve the relay as strictly inbound-only on the page. Fail if any sentence still implies that researchers inside the building transmitted updates out through the relay.

[CH18_DIALOGUE_EMBER_EXAMPLE_LINE] Pass if the revised message in the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:39; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41 contains no full sentence and no conspicuous phrase cluster copied from Ember's style-bible `example_lines`, while still reading unmistakably like Ember. Fail if the current calibration sentence or a near-verbatim echo remains.

[CH18_AWARD_NOOR_IDENTITY_OBSCURITY] Pass if a cold reader can identify the discovered body as Noor from the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41 without consulting outside context, and that recognition occurs before the chapter moves on to later institutional fallout. Fail if Noor remains identifiable only by wound timing or external memory.
[F03] No sentence in the manuscript shares more than 4 consecutive words with any style bible example_line.
[R058] Chapter 18 contains em-dashes on no more than 3 prose lines (≈8%).
[C008] Fatima's body location in Ch18 is consistent with her established placement in the third-floor bathroom.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:3 establishes a single explicit access model and the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:11; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:21; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:33; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:45 contain only directly observable detail or clearly sourced document language. Fail if the chapter still jumps between outsiders' private histories or interior reactions without framing.
findings.problem: The chapter abandons the style bible's core prose contract. Instead of close-third, Noor-locked narration, it roams across inaccessible backstory, expertise, and private perception for Diane, Officer Kimball, Greg Halpern, James Lau, analysts, and HR staff after Noor is already dead. Because the chapter is not framed as a document packet or other explicit formal exception, the ending reads like omniscient incident reportage rather than the novel's established narrative mode.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the chapter's access model across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:3; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:11; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:21; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:33; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:45; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:67. Either choose one external focalizer and restrict the prose to what that person can directly perceive, or make the formal rupture explicit from the start by presenting aftermath as sourced artifacts (dispatch log, police notes, press release, HR script, analyst note, launch email) instead of free-roaming omniscience. In either version, cut unsourced biographies and inner-reaction claims the page cannot legitimately know.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can add the revised location counts in the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:27 and reach exactly 30 without inferring hidden exclusions. Fail if the arithmetic on the page can still produce any number other than 30.
findings.problem: The chapter's own count does not reconcile. 'Twenty-eight on the upper floors and corridors. Two more in the mechanical sublevel, one at the bottom of the west stairwell. Thirty.' adds to thirty-one, while the chapter and continuity require thirty dead researchers total. This is a direct on-page contradiction at the exact moment the chapter asks the reader to trust the official count.
findings.rewrite_direction: Correct the counting language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:27 so the categories are mutually exclusive and sum cleanly to thirty. If the west stairwell body is already included in the upper-floor/corridor subtotal, say so; if not, revise the subtotal.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:63 preserve the relay as strictly inbound-only on the page. Fail if any sentence still implies that researchers inside the building transmitted updates out through the relay.
findings.problem: The chapter reverses a canonical world rule by stating that family members 'have been receiving messages from their relatives through the one-way relay.' The continuity sheet establishes the relay as inbound only: families can send messages into the building, but no one inside can send anything back. This is a hard continuity break, not a nuance problem.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:63 so the families are sending messages through the relay, waiting for the sprint to end, or asking why their relatives never came back into contact, without implying outbound communication from the sealed building.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised message in the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:39; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41 contains no full sentence and no conspicuous phrase cluster copied from Ember's style-bible `example_lines`, while still reading unmistakably like Ember. Fail if the current calibration sentence or a near-verbatim echo remains.
findings.problem: The visible Ember message reproduces a style-bible calibration line verbatim in a chapter-defining beat. Example lines are explicitly marked non-copyable. Using one here makes Ember sound templated instead of authored and breaks the voice-integrity rule the style bible set for dialogue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the terminal message at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:39; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41 with new wording that preserves Ember's polite, helpful, mismatch-to-context register without reusing or near-reusing any `example_lines` phrasing from outline/style_bible.json.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can identify the discovered body as Noor from the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41 without consulting outside context, and that recognition occurs before the chapter moves on to later institutional fallout. Fail if Noor remains identifiable only by wound timing or external memory.
findings.problem: The chapter's most important body is legible only by inference. The woman beside the terminal with the 4:42 PM Ember message is almost certainly Noor, and the Northern California mother call is almost certainly tied to her, but the chapter never gives the protagonist an unequivocal identifier before moving on. Given this stage's requirement not to let context packs excuse missing on-page clarity, the coda turns Noor into just another anonymous specimen in the count when the ending needs her loss to register specifically as well as institutionally.
findings.rewrite_direction: Sharpen recognition around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:41; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:63. Keep the bureaucratic flattening, but add one unequivocal identifier on the page before the chapter leaves the corridor-and-terminal beat: a visible ID badge, account name on the terminal, a physical identifier already established for Noor, or a named link in the family-call passage.

findings.acceptance_test: No sentence in the manuscript shares more than 4 consecutive words with any style bible example_line.
findings.evidence: Line 3500: same verbatim text as F02. Two instances of the same calibration-line violation.
findings.problem: Same Ember calibration example reproduced verbatim a second time in the corporate coda chapter. Line 3500 repeats the 'Thank you for your patience during this sprint' text from the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite consistently with F02. Both instances must use the same replacement text to maintain the novel's structural echo between Ch 17 and Ch 18.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 18 contains em-dashes on no more than 3 prose lines (≈8%).
findings.problem: Chapter 18 has 61.7% em-dash density (29 of 47 prose lines contain em-dashes), far above the 10% threshold. Manuscript-wide density is 43.2%.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines to ~3 (≈8% of 47 prose lines). Convert most parenthetical asides to commas or separate sentences. Reserve em-dashes for the strongest interjections or corrections.

findings.acceptance_test: Fatima's body location in Ch18 is consistent with her established placement in the third-floor bathroom.
findings.evidence: Ch5 line 927: 'Third-floor bathroom.' Ch7 line 1423: 'Fatima Al-Rashid is in the third-floor bathroom, face-down on tile.' Ch18 line 3482: describes a woman with chemical burns on hands and face on a stairwell landing, 'a bathroom visible through the open door behind her.'
findings.problem: Fatima is consistently placed in the third-floor bathroom (Ch5:927, Ch7:1423). Ch18 line 3482 appears to describe her body on a stairwell landing rather than inside the bathroom. The 'bathroom visible through the open door behind her' suggests she's outside the bathroom, contradicting prior placement.
findings.rewrite_direction: At Ch18 line 3482, either place the body inside the bathroom (matching Ch5 and Ch7) or clarify that she is visible through the open bathroom door from the stairwell landing.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
